title: eriogonum vimineum var. baileyi
intro: Eriogonum vimineum var. baileyi, also known as Bailey's wire-lettuce, is a variety of shrub-like plant that belongs to the buckwheat family (Polygonaceae). The plant is known for its medicinal properties, particularly in its antiseptic, anti-inflammatory, and antimicrobial qualities, which can be attributed to its high concentration of flavonoids and phenolic acids. The health benefits of Eriogonum vimineum var. baileyi include its potential to reduce inflammation, improve wound healing, and protect against oxidative stress, making it a valuable remedy for various skin conditions and infections. Traditionally, the plant is prepared as a tea, tincture, or salve, which can be used topically to treat skin irritations and wounds, or taken internally to promote overall well-being.
